Name.description: The name of the event bus. Currently, this is always default.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the account permitted to write events to the current account.

KmsKeyIdentifier.description:
The identifier of the KMS customer managed key for EventBridge to use to encrypt events on this event bus, if one has been specified.

For more information, see Data encryption in EventBridge in the Amazon EventBridge User Guide.

DeadLetterConfig.description:
Configuration details of the Amazon SQS queue for EventBridge to use as a dead-letter queue (DLQ).

For more information, see Using dead-letter queues to process undelivered events in the EventBridge User Guide.

Policy.description: The policy that enables the external account to send events to your account.

LogConfig.description:
The logging configuration settings for the event bus.

For more information, see Configuring logs for event buses in the EventBridge User Guide.

CreationTime.description: The time the event bus was created.

LastModifiedTime.description: The time the event bus was last modified.

ApiDestinations.description: An array that includes information about each API destination.

NextToken.description:
A token indicating there are more results available. If there are no more results, no token is included in the response.

The value of nextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. To retrieve the next page of results, make the call again using the returned token. Keep all other arguments unchanged.

Using an expired pagination token results in an HTTP 400 InvalidToken error.

Archives.description: An array of Archive objects that include details about an archive.

The value of nextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. To retrieve the next page of results, make the call again using the returned token. Keep all other arguments unchanged.

Using an expired pagination token results in an HTTP 400 InvalidToken error.

Connections.description: An array of connections objects that include details about the connections.

Replays.description: An array of Replay objects that contain information about the replay.

Rules.description: The rules that match the specified criteria.

NextToken.description:
A token indicating there are more results available. If there are no more results, no token is included in the response.

ApiDestinationArn.description: The ARN of the API destination retrieved.

Name.description: The name of the API destination retrieved.

Description.description: The description for the API destination retrieved.

ApiDestinationState.description: The state of the API destination retrieved.

ConnectionArn.description: The ARN of the connection specified for the API destination retrieved.

InvocationEndpoint.description: The URL to use to connect to the HTTP endpoint.

HttpMethod.description: The method to use to connect to the HTTP endpoint.

InvocationRateLimitPerSecond.description: The maximum number of invocations per second to specified for the API destination. Note that if you set the invocation rate maximum to a value lower the rate necessary to send all events received on to the destination HTTP endpoint, some events may not be delivered within the 24-hour retry window. If you plan to set the rate lower than the rate necessary to deliver all events, consider using a dead-letter queue to catch events that are not delivered within 24 hours.

CreationTime.description: A time stamp for the time that the API destination was created.

LastModifiedTime.description: A time stamp for the time that the API destination was last modified.

EventSourceArn.description: The ARN of the event source associated with the archive.

EventPattern.description: The event pattern used to filter events sent to the archive.

StateReason.description: The reason that the archive is in the state.

KmsKeyIdentifier.description:
The identifier of the KMS customer managed key for EventBridge to use to encrypt this archive, if one has been specified.

For more information, see Encrypting archives in the Amazon EventBridge User Guide.

RetentionDays.description: The number of days to retain events for in the archive.

SizeBytes.description: The size of the archive in bytes.

EventCount.description: The number of events in the archive.

CreationTime.description: The time at which the archive was created.

InvocationConnectivityParameters.description:
For connections to private APIs The parameters EventBridge uses to invoke the resource endpoint.

For more information, see Connecting to private APIs in the Amazon EventBridge User Guide .

StateReason.description: The reason that the connection is in the current connection state.

AuthorizationType.description: The type of authorization specified for the connection.

SecretArn.description: The ARN of the secret created from the authorization parameters specified for the connection.

KmsKeyIdentifier.description:
The identifier of the KMS customer managed key for EventBridge to use to encrypt the connection, if one has been specified.

For more information, see Encrypting connections in the Amazon EventBridge User Guide.

AuthParameters.description: The parameters to use for authorization for the connection.

CreationTime.description: A time stamp for the time that the connection was created.

LastModifiedTime.description: A time stamp for the time that the connection was last modified.

LastAuthorizedTime.description: A time stamp for the time that the connection was last authorized.

Name.description: The name of the endpoint you asked for information about.

Description.description: The description of the endpoint you asked for information about.

Arn.description: The ARN of the endpoint you asked for information about.

RoutingConfig.description: The routing configuration of the endpoint you asked for information about.

ReplicationConfig.description: Whether replication is enabled or disabled for the endpoint you asked for information about.

EventBuses.description: The event buses being used by the endpoint you asked for information about.

RoleArn.description: The ARN of the role used by the endpoint you asked for information about.

EndpointId.description: The ID of the endpoint you asked for information about.

EndpointUrl.description: The URL of the endpoint you asked for information about.

State.description: The current state of the endpoint you asked for information about.

StateReason.description: The reason the endpoint you asked for information about is in its current state.

CreationTime.description: The time the endpoint you asked for information about was created.

LastModifiedTime.description: The last time the endpoint you asked for information about was modified.

Arn.description: The ARN of the partner event source.

CreatedBy.description: The name of the SaaS partner that created the event source.

CreationTime.description: The date and time that the event source was created.

ExpirationTime.description: The date and time that the event source will expire if you do not create a matching event bus.

Name.description: The name of the partner event source.

State.description: The state of the event source. If it is ACTIVE, you have already created a matching event bus for this event source, and that event bus is active. If it is PENDING, either you haven't yet created a matching event bus, or that event bus is deactivated. If it is DELETED, you have created a matching event bus, but the event source has since been deleted.

StateReason.description: The reason that the replay is in the current state.

EventSourceArn.description: The ARN of the archive events were replayed from.

Destination.description: A ReplayDestination object that contains details about the replay.

EventStartTime.description: The time stamp of the first event that was last replayed from the archive.

EventEndTime.description: The time stamp for the last event that was replayed from the archive.

EventLastReplayedTime.description: The time that the event was last replayed.

ReplayStartTime.description: A time stamp for the time that the replay started.

ReplayEndTime.description: A time stamp for the time that the replay stopped.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the rule.

EventPattern.description: The event pattern. For more information, see Events and Event Patterns in the Amazon EventBridge User Guide .

State.description: Specifies whether the rule is enabled or disabled.

RoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role associated with the rule.

ManagedBy.description: If this is a managed rule, created by an Amazon Web Services service on your behalf, this field displays the principal name of the Amazon Web Services service that created the rule.

EventBusName.description: The name of the event bus associated with the rule.

CreatedBy.description: The account ID of the user that created the rule. If you use PutRule to put a rule on an event bus in another account, the other account is the owner of the rule, and the rule ARN includes the account ID for that account. However, the value for CreatedBy is the account ID as the account that created the rule in the other account.

PartnerEventSourceAccounts.description: The list of partner event sources returned by the operation.

NextToken.description:
A token indicating there are more results available. If there are no more results, no token is included in the response.

The value of nextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. To retrieve the next page of results, make the call again using the returned token. Keep all other arguments unchanged.

Using an expired pagination token results in an HTTP 400 InvalidToken error.

PartnerEventSources.description: The list of partner event sources returned by the operation.
